scope: Complete end-to-end runtime audit, hardening, traceability, fixes, and coverage uplift for runtime-owned behavior.

notes: This matrix is the controlling traceability artifact for the single-branch runtime hardening pass.
Rows with status audit-required are inventory rows that still need source tracing, coverage evidence, and issue triage.
Rows with status fixed require the named validation commands to remain green before merge readiness can be claimed.

commands: rg -n "TODO|FIXME|HACK|XXX|not implemented|Not implemented|NOT IMPLEMENTED|stub|placeholder|fake success" runtime/src runtime/tests --glob '!**/dist/**' --glob '!**/node_modules/**'

results.coverage: failed with exit code -1 before writing coverage-summary.json; partial V8 temp files were written under runtime/coverage/.tmp

rows.behavior: CLI, daemon, package binary, built-dist import, package entrypoint, and runtime asset startup paths work from source and from built artifacts.
edgeCases: starts after a clean build with package assets present
reports missing packaged assets as a failing validation
daemon startup and foreground startup surface actionable errors

rows.behavior: Session submit, turn execution, phase orchestration, tool loop, compaction decisions, cancellation, pending input, and turn completion produce correct state and events.
edgeCases: empty input with no pending input is a no-op
pending mailbox input is drained into the next model turn
tool results force continuation when the model still needs follow-up
aborts and provider errors close the turn boundary
validationCommands: npm --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime test -- tests/session tests/phases tests/services/compact tests/recovery

rows.behavior: When a child agent completes, its final notification wakes a hidden parent follow-up turn so the user receives a response without sending another message.
edgeCases: direct child completion queues triggerTurn=true and calls parent submit with displayUserMessage null
root-thread inter-agent communication with triggerTurn=true starts a hidden root follow-up
missing submit hooks do not crash standalone unit fakes

rows.behavior: Agent spawn, wait, send message, follow-up, close, interrupt, resume, mailbox routing, task bridge, and agent job orchestration work end to end.
edgeCases: wait returns completed child findings and drains only the intended mailbox content
interruption and shutdown propagate to descendants without orphaned work
resume restores open descendants and rejects stale handles
agent job retries and final result reporting preserve ordering

rows.behavior: Tool dispatch, approval prompts, permission modes, guardian review, yolo classification, and policy enforcement are correct across allowed, denied, rewritten, aborted, and errored paths.
edgeCases: dangerous tool allow rules do not bypass approval semantics
denied tool use returns visible recovery output
yolo mode classifies package prompt assets after build
hook rewrites preserve the approval prompt shown to the user
validationCommands: npm --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime test -- tests/tools tests/permissions tests/sandbox tests/hooks

rows.behavior: Provider selection, model registry, model switches, streaming adapters, retry behavior, credentials, service tier, and provider-specific errors are routed correctly.
edgeCases: provider switch resets continuation state
credential failures are visible and actionable
streaming adapters preserve final text, usage, and tool calls
service tier validation fails before spawning child work
validationCommands: npm --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime test -- tests/llm tests/services/api tests/auth tests/config tests/commands/provider.test.ts

rows.behavior: Rollout persistence, session store, thread store, state migrations, background agent runner, background tasks, resume, and compaction survive restart and partial failure paths.
edgeCases: rollout reconstruction handles interrupted turns
restored agents rebind lifecycle events
state migrations are idempotent
background tasks terminate cleanly on kill or process failure
validationCommands: npm --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime test -- tests/session tests/state tests/app-server tests/tasks

rows.behavior: Runtime events that feed the TUI transcript, tasks, permissions, agents, status notices, and workbench UX are structured, ordered, bounded, and rendered without hiding required user-facing outcomes.
edgeCases: raw collab tool rows do not suppress required final user-facing content
wide terminal output remains bounded to content width
permission and tool-result events remain visible after resume
runtime event ordering survives daemon buffering
validationCommands: npm --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime test -- tests/tui/parity tests/tui/state tests/tui/workbench tests/app-server

rows.behavior: Runtime-owned code has meaningful coverage evidence, dead-code reports are reviewed, skipped tests are accounted for, and incomplete-code scans are clean or tracked to fixed rows.
edgeCases: coverage reports include runtime-owned source and tests
known generated or external-only exclusions are explicit
skipped tests are listed and triaged
dead-code findings are fixed or mapped to concrete rows
fixesNeeded: Triage why the full coverage command exits with code -1 and leaves only partial V8 temp files.
Classify the 1019 incomplete-marker matches into test fixture text, historical comments, and real implementation debt.
Triage the 7 skipped-or-todo test matches and record which are platform guards versus real coverage gaps.
Inspect the two silent catch candidates in runtime/src/services/api/openaiShim.ts and either justify or fix them with tests.
